open_positions.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
open_positions.thesis: no mechanism. Catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-01 with Items 2.05 (restructuring) and 7.01 (Reg FD disclosure). Falsified by: n/a.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: Entered UNDERDONE conf 3 on restructuring 8-K (2.05 + 7.01) filed 2026-06-01. Stock has now rallied +7.38% today and +16.21% over 5 days, consistent with the thesis that a standalone restructuring filing (not paired with earnings miss) signals proactive cost discipline and is read bullishly by SaaS investors. The 7.01 Reg FD disclosure likely contained guidance reaffirmation or framing that justified the gap up. Unrealized P&L is now flat to slightly positive on a 3-day hold. Catalyst is fresh and thesis remains intact — the rally is the expected grading of a correct read. Volume spike (1.33x) validates participation. No news since entry that would materially change the setup. Hold through the week to see if follow-through thesis (analyst upgrades, further SaaS sector relative strength) materializes.

open_positions.latest_reasoning: Entered UNDERDONE conf 3 on 2026-06-04 same-day 8-K with 5.02 (officer/director), 5.03 (charter), 5.07 (shareholder vote) — read as annual meeting boilerplate, not material negative, despite XBI -3.6% sector selloff. Position is flat on 0 days held (same-day entry), unrealized P&L -1.27%. The new flag now re-classifies the same 8-K as OVERDONE conf 3, based on reading the items as routine board elections (5.02) and not executive departures. This is actually confirmatory of the original UNDERDONE thesis: the market oversold on risk-off tape and meeting-results boilerplate. Since the new flag's analysis explicitly validates the buy thesis (5.02 is routine election, not departure), hold the position. Horizon is days; if 8-K exhibits confirm director-only elections with no pipeline news, the fade thesis is high-conviction.

closed_positions.name: Best Buy Co., Inc.
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Original thesis was OVERDONE conf-3 fade (days horizon); position entered at cost 74.074, current 72.78 (unrealized -1.81% after 4 days). New flag today re-surfaces identical OVERDONE catalyst (Q1 earnings 8-K 5/28, continuation narrative) at conf 3, but five-day move has now extended to +27.47% on 2.24x volume while XLY sits negative—classic squeeze/positioning overlay on genuine beat. Exit reasoning from 0 days ago explicitly called for a fade retrace within 5-10 days. Trimming all 4 shares locks in the +3.19% realized gain already achieved and de-risks the remaining beta to what remains a structurally weak discretionary setup (tariff exposure, CE demand pressure). Holding zero BBY aligns with the higher-conviction fade thesis already in motion. (partial)

closed_positions.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Entry thesis was OVERDONE (conf 3, weeks horizon) on Iran missile headline + defense sector bid. Position is -4.56% unrealized after 3 days. New flag today re-confirms OVERDONE conf 3 with identical catalyst, but now the five-day move is 30.83% on 3.28x volume, with no company-specific 8-K or contract award. The thesis has NOT evolved—it's the same geopolitical headline bid. If the setup was OVERDONE three days ago at a 10-15% run, it is definitively OVERDONE now at 30%+ on no new catalyst. Historical precedent in defense mid-caps is a 30-50% retrace within two weeks absent a contract award. Thesis is broken because the move has overextended past the point where geopolitical upside can justify hold. EXIT fully and avoid the retrace.

closed_positions.name: Advanced Energy Industries
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Re-evaluation required: original entry was OVERDONE conf 3 on 8-K surprise, closed 4 days ago at -6.48% realized loss. New flag re-lists AEIS as OVERDONE conf 3 with 1.01/2.03/3.02 financing signature and sector beta narrative. However, I explicitly exited this position 4 days ago because thesis was killed: the move was pure semicap-sector beta (1.6x SMH), not idiosyncratic catalyst, and conviction was gone. The new flag argues financing terms (convert premium, use of proceeds) could be priced in—plausible, but it is the SAME 8-K event I already saw on exit day (timestamp 5/13), and the re-entry bar is NOT met: confidence is still 3 (re-entry requires ≥4), and there is NO genuinely new information (the 8-K itself was the prior-exit catalyst; flag just re-describes it with added thesis flavor about financing terms). Thesis was killed by sector beta; flag does not supply new information to resurrect it. Exit to stop further drift and redeploy cash.

closed_positions.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Original entry was OVERDONE conf 3 on 8-K surprise narrative. New flag reclassifies as RATIONAL conf 3 and provides 8-K URL (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/927003/000110465926060543/aeis-20260513x8k.htm) confirming two consecutive 8.01 filings are 'likely investor conference / presentation materials'—benign. Thesis killed: the move (-6.13% new vs -7.96% realized) is pure sector beta (1.6x SMH), not an idiosyncratic catalyst. Position down -7.96% after 2 days with no directional edge remaining. Conviction gone; exit to redeploy capital.

closed_positions.name: Extreme Networks
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Entered on thesis that EXTR was experiencing sympathy selling off CALX's -14% print—a short-term dislocations trade with 'days' horizon. 8 days in, EXTR has recovered +19% while XLK remains elevated, indicating the read-through trade has normalized and the catalyst (CALX weakness dragging peers) has exhausted its effect. Holding further exposes the portfolio to reversal risk without a fresh catalyst. The original high-confidence short-term mismatch has been arbitraged away. No evidence of broader enterprise IT weakness that would sustain further upside. Exit captures the gain and redeploys capital toward fresh opportunities.
